author: y4rr
content:
Result: wired the previously-orphaned pin_memory flag through the DataLoader transfer pipeline so it actually triggers the pinned-memory CPU→GPU path.

Three layers of changes:

1. **Tensor::to_pinned(device)** in ferrotorch-core/src/tensor.rs — new method that uses the existing TensorStorage::on_device_pinned for the CPU→CUDA case (which calls the GpuBackend::cpu_to_gpu_pinned trait method, ultimately landing in ferrotorch_gpu::transfer::cpu_to_gpu_pinned which allocates a temporary pinned host buffer and DMAs to device). All other paths (CPU→CPU, CUDA→CPU, cross-GPU) fall through to the regular .to() since pinned memory only helps for CPU→GPU H2D.

2. **ToDevice trait extension** in ferrotorch-data/src/dataloader.rs — added a new `to_device_pinned(device)` method to the ToDevice trait, defaulting to forwarding to to_device for back-compat with existing impls. Also added a blanket impl for Tensor<T> so loaders that yield raw tensors work without a wrapper struct.

3. **DataLoader transfer pipeline** — changed TransferFn signature from `Fn(Vec<S>) -> ...` to `Fn(Vec<S>, bool) -> ...` where the bool is the pin_memory flag, so the closure built by .device() can branch on the flag at call time (not at construction time). Both the synchronous DataLoaderIter and the prefetch PrefetchIter now thread the pin_memory flag through to the transfer call. This means pin_memory(true) and device() can be called in any order.

Tests: 7 new pin_memory tests verify the dispatch is correct without needing a real CUDA device — the test DeviceSample tracks which method (`to_device` vs `to_device_pinned`) was called via a Some(bool) field. Coverage:
- Default (pin_memory off) -> to_device path
- pin_memory(true) + sync iter -> to_device_pinned
- pin_memory(true) + prefetch iter -> to_device_pinned
- pin_memory set BEFORE device() takes effect
- pin_memory set AFTER device() takes effect (regression check on the runtime flag plumbing)
- pin_memory without device() is a no-op
- ToDevice with no to_device_pinned override falls back to to_device via the default forwarder

All 147 ferrotorch-data lib tests pass (was 140, +7). 878 ferrotorch-core lib tests pass. No new warnings.
